sections.paragraph: ὅμοιος, α, ον [akin to ὁμός ‘one and the same, common’] similar (to), like most freq. w. dat. of the entity used as base for comparison Mt 11:16 al.; w. gen. J 8:55 v.l. The acc. in Rv 1:13 and 14:14 may be understood adv.: ὅμοιον υἱὸν ἀνϑρώπου in likeness a human being. W. focus on some feature in the object of comparison Mt 22:39 and Mk 12:31 v.l. (level of importance); Rv 13:4 (intrinsic value); 18:18 (prestige).
